summary: Static code analysis + logic unit tests verified the reservation overlap fix in ReservationForm.tsx. All 4 overlap comparisons (checkAvailability reservations+maintenance, handleSubmit reservations+maintenance) correctly use strict < and > instead of <= and >=, enabling same-day turnaround while blocking true overlaps.
critical_code_review_comments: ReservationForm.tsx is 1576 lines - exceeds 700-line threshold. Consider splitting into smaller modules (availability check, form fields, submission handler).
Overlap logic is duplicated across 4 locations (lines 303, 340, 649, 683). Extract into a reusable helper function `hasDateOverlap(aStart, aEnd, bStart, bEnd)` to prevent future divergence.

Availability check is client-side only - a race condition or bypass could still create overlapping reservations. Recommend adding a DB-level exclusion constraint (Postgres tstzrange + GIST) or Supabase RPC for atomic validation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Overlap fix verified via static code review + 7 logic scenarios (same-day turnaround allowed, exact-match blocked, mid-overlap blocked, containment blocked both ways, gap allowed). Live end-to-end testing not performed because Supabase RLS blocks unauthenticated queries. If auth credentials become available, run a Playwright test: create reservation July 11-14, then attempt July 14-17 (should succeed) and July 13-17 (should be blocked with toast 'NO SE PUEDE RESERVAR').
rca of the issue: Previous code used `newStart <= rEnd && newEnd >= rStart` which treated the boundary day (July 14) as overlapping. Fix uses strict `<` and `>` so a reservation ending on July 14 does not conflict with one starting on July 14. Verified in all 4 code paths.
